1.description: This figure displays the four steps required to create a saving goal on the app. The four steps are as follows: (Panel A) naming the goal; (Panel B) choosing between the six categories Hobby (Tempo Libero), Travel (Viaggi), Other (Altro), Car (Veicoli), House (Casa), and Savings (Risparmio); (Panel C) choosing a target amount (e.g., € 3,000) and a deadline (e.g., 30/08/2019); and (Panel D) linking the goal to an image. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

2.description: This figure displays the sections of the app where users choose the mutual fund for their savings (Panel A), monitor goal progression (Panels B and C) and pledge money with the “Joink” function (Panel D). [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

3.description: This figure displays the cross-sectional distribution of four key features of the saving goals set by users. The top panels show the horizon of each goal, computed as the difference (in days) between the deadline of the goal and the day the goal was set. Because the distribution features a thin right tail, we display it in two parts: The upper-left panel is associated with horizons shorter than 1,000 days while the upper-right panel is associated with horizons longer than 1,000 days. The middle panels report the amounts (in €) that users set as their saving target. The left panel corresponds to goals less than or equal to € 10,000, while the right panel corresponds to goals greater than € 10,000. The bottom-left panel displays the type of funds users invest their savings in: Fixed Income, Balanced, and Equity funds. The bottom-right panel displays the categories of goals that users can choose from: Hobby, Travel, Other, Car, House, and Savings. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

4.description: This figure displays the relation between goal characteristics and the risk of the mutual funds associated with them. Panel A (Panel B) displays the relation between risk and goal horizon (goal amount). Goal horizon is computed as the difference (in days) between the deadline of the goal and the day the goal was set. Risk is measured as the annualized standard deviation of the fund's returns. The blue lines denote the nearest-neighbor estimate of the conditional mean of risk, while the red dashed lines denote 95% confidence intervals. Panel C displays the average risk (with 95% confidence interval) by goal category. Panel D displays the breakdown of goal categories by type of fund (Fixed Income, Equity, and Balanced). [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

5.description: This figure displays the dynamics of goal creation (Panel A) and the dynamics of saving when individuals set goals (Panel B). In Panel A, for each app user, we only consider the sample starting in October 2017, just after goals were introduced. We then standardize the length of time users are active on the app from zero to one and plot the percentage of individuals who have set at least one goal since starting to use the app (solid blue line) and the percentage of individuals who have at least one goal open at a given time (blue dashed line). The solid and dashed green lines repeat the exercise but focus on app users with at least two goals. In Panel B, the sample consists of the 14,521 goals with a deadline before the date we obtained the data (November 2, 2019). To ease comparison across goals with different characteristics, we normalize to one both the horizon and the amount of each goal. For each goal, we then cumulate the amount deposited by the user over time so that we have the percentage of the goal completed as a function of the percentage of the time elapsed. Finally, we compute the average percentage completion across all goals as a function of the time elapsed. The black line corresponds to all goals, the blue line to attained goals, and the green line to unattained goals. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

6.description: This figure displays the time-series evolution across beta testers and nonbeta testers for monthly net deposits (Panel A), number of transactions (Panel B), portfolio risk (Panel C), and number of accounts (Panel D). The blue lines plot average values for beta testers and the red lines average values for non beta testers. We also report the coefficient estimate (and associatedt$t$-statistic) of theρ1$\rho _1$coefficient from the regressionyi,t=πi+ρ0·t+ρ1·t·Di+εi,t$y_{i,t}=\pi _i+\rho _0\cdot t +\rho _1 \cdot t \cdot D_i+\epsilon _{i,t}$, whereyi,t$y_{i,t}$is the economic variable of interest,πi$\pi _i$represents user fixed effects,ρ0$\rho _0$is the coefficient on the slope for nonbeta testers with respect to a time trend, andρ1$\rho _1$is the coefficient on the difference in slopes with respect to a time trend for the beta testers and the nonbeta testers. The null hypothesis of parallel trends corresponds toρ1=0$\rho _1=0$. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

7.description: This figure displays the dynamic relation between goal setting and saving. Specifically, the figure displays theβs$\beta _s$coefficients and the corresponding 95% confidence intervals from the panel regressionM_Net_Depositsi,t=αi+αt+∑s=−618βsTarget_Dummyi,s,t+εi,t$M\_Net\_Deposits_{i,t}=\alpha _{i}+\alpha _{t}+\sum\limits_{s=-6}^{18}\beta _s \ Target\_Dummy_{i,s,t}+\epsilon _{i,t}$.whereM_Net_Depositsi,t$M\_Net\_Deposits_{i,t}$is the monthly net deposits (i.e., the difference between total monthly deposits and withdrawals) of useri$i$at timet$t$,αi$\alpha _{i}$andαt$\alpha _{t}$are user and time fixed effects, andTarget_Dummyi,s,t$Target\_Dummy_{i,s,t}$is a dummy variable equal to one if useri$i$is using a target for thethmonth in the calendar montht$t$. Standard errors are double-clustered at the user and month levels. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

8.description: This figure displays the distribution of answers to survey questions about users' saving behavior outside the app before and after its adoption. The question associated with Panel A is“Since using the App, have you changed how much you saved outside the App?”The questions associated with Panel B are“Before using the App, how much have you saved on average per month outside the App?”(blue bars) and“Since using the App, how much have you saved on average per month outside the App?”(red bars). We display the percentage of respondents at the top of each bar. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

9.description: This figure displays the distribution of answers to survey questions about the use of saving goals. The question associated with Panel A is“In your experience, what is the most motivating aspect of having a saving goal?”The question associated with Panel B is“Think about the saving goals you have achieved. In your opinion, what has been the most decisive factor?”The question associated with Panel C is“Think about the saving goals you have NOT achieved. In your opinion, what has been the most decisive factor?”We display the percentage of respondents at the top of each bar. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]
